content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions or states that took place before a specific point in the past: By the time the guests arrived, we **had already set** the table. She **had worked** at the company for ten years before she became manager.
Past actions whose results were relevant at a particular moment in the past: The players were celebrating because they **had won** the match.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: The past perfect simple is often used with the following words and phrases:
rows: I'd finished my homework **by** eight o'clock.
**By the time** I got to class, the lesson had started.
The teacher had checked the answers **before** the lesson.
I left **after** I'd finished the test.
Simon had **just** finished the test when the bell rang.
I left **when** I'd finished the test.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: Choosing between the past simple and the past perfect simple changes the order of events.
The show started when we arrived. (= We arrived first, then the show started.)
The show had started when we arrived. (= The show started first, then we arrived.)
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms.

content.subtitle: Use
items: Activities that were in progress up to, or until just before, a moment in the past: He was out of breath because he **had been running**. They deserved a rest — they **had been working** all morning.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: The past perfect continuous is often used with the following words and phrases:
rows: Tony had been studying **for** hours, so he had a headache.
She'd been hoping to win the competition **since** the summer.
We'd been talking about the Internet **before** the lesson started.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: The past perfect simple often emphasises the result of an action: ✓ She'd **written** an article for the school newspaper. (= She'd finished it.)

The past perfect continuous often emphasises the action, and the time spent on the action, rather than the result: ✓ She'd **been writing** an article for the newspaper. (= She'd started, but she hadn't finished it.)

sections.title: A: Past perfect simple
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past perfect simple form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: When we got to the station, the train ...... (already / leave).
Mia ...... (finish) her homework before dinner.
They ...... (not / book) a table, so the restaurant was full.
...... (Tom / ever / fly) before he went to Canada?
The match started after ...... (the referee / check) the pitch.
I couldn't buy a ticket because ...... (I / leave) my wallet at home.
...... (you / already / finish) lunch when I arrived?

sections.title: B: Choose the sentence with the same meaning
sections.instructions: Choose the sentence (A or B) which means the same as the first sentence.

items.context: We had cleaned the kitchen when Mum came home.
items.sentenceA: Mum came home and then we cleaned the kitchen.
items.sentenceB: We cleaned the kitchen and then Mum came home.

items.context: I called Sam after I had finished my project.
items.sentenceA: I finished my project and then I called Sam.
items.sentenceB: I called Sam and then I finished my project.

items.context: By the time the film started, we'd bought popcorn.
items.sentenceA: We bought popcorn before the film started.
items.sentenceB: We bought popcorn after the film started.

items.context: Lily didn't leave until her brother had arrived.
items.sentenceA: Lily left before her brother arrived.
items.sentenceB: Her brother arrived and then Lily left.

items.context: The teacher hadn't marked the tests by Friday.
items.sentenceA: The tests were still unmarked on Friday.
items.sentenceB: The teacher marked the tests before Friday.

items.context: They had sold the bike before I phoned about it.
items.sentenceA: They sold the bike and later I phoned.
items.sentenceB: I phoned and then they sold the bike.

items.context: Dad had cooked dinner when we arrived.
items.sentenceA: We arrived and then Dad cooked dinner.
items.sentenceB: Dad cooked dinner and then we arrived.

sections.title: C: Write sentences using the prompts
sections.instructions: Write sentences using the prompts. One of the verbs must be in the past perfect simple.

items.sentence: they / already / leave / when / we / arrive
items.answer: They had already left when we arrived.

items.sentence: I / not / meet / Anna / before / she / join / our class
items.answer: I hadn't met Anna before she joined our class.

items.sentence: when / Dad / get home / we / already / eat / dinner
items.answer: When Dad got home, we had already eaten dinner.

items.sentence: he / be / tired / because / he / not / sleep / well
items.answer: He was tired because he hadn't slept well.

items.sentence: by the time / the concert / begin / everyone / find / their seats
items.answer: By the time the concert began, everyone had found their seats.

sections.title: D: Past perfect continuous
sections.instructions: Look at the pictures and complete the sentences. Use the correct form of the past perfect continuous.

items.sentence: He was covered in paint because ...... (paint / the fence).
items.answer: he had been painting the fence

items.sentence: They were exhausted because ...... (walk / all morning).
items.answer: they had been walking all morning

items.sentence: The streets were wet because ...... (it / rain).
Did she lose her voice because ...... (sing / for hours)?
items.answer: she had been singing for hours

items.sentence: When the bus came, ...... (we / wait / for forty minutes).
items.answer: we had been waiting for forty minutes

items.sentence: When I called, ...... (they / not / study / long).
I'd only ...... for ten minutes when the phone rang.
Had they already ...... the tickets before you called?
She hadn't ...... us about the change, so we were surprised.
We had ...... football for an hour before it started raining.
Mark had ...... his room all morning, so it looked much better.
It was my first time abroad because I'd never ...... to another country before.

sections.title: F: Find the extra word
sections.instructions: If a line is correct, click OK. If there is an extra word in a line, click on it.

items.sentence: Yesterday our team finally [had] won the school basketball final.
We had [been] trained hard for weeks, so everyone was excited.
Before the game started, the coach [had] gave us a short talk.
The other team scored first, but we didn't give up.
By half-time, we had [been] scored three points.
I was tired because I had been running up and down the court all morning.
When the referee blew the final whistle, everyone shouted with joy.
Our captain had prepared a speech before the match.
[Been] After we had collected our medals, we took photos.
By the time we got home, Dad had [yet] told all our neighbours!

content.subtitle: Use
items: Actions or states that took place before a specific point in the past: By the time the guests arrived, we **had already set** the table. She **had worked** at the company for ten years before she became manager.
Past actions whose results were relevant at a particular moment in the past: The players were celebrating because they **had won** the match.

rows: I'd finished my homework **by** eight o'clock.
**By the time** I got to class, the lesson had started.
The teacher had checked the answers **before** the lesson.
I left **after** I'd finished the test.
Simon had **just** finished the test when the bell rang.
I left **when** I'd finished the test.

notes: Choosing between the past simple and the past perfect simple changes the order of events.
The show started when we arrived. (= We arrived first, then the show started.)
The show had started when we arrived. (= The show started first, then we arrived.)
Some verbs have irregular past participle forms.

items: Activities that were in progress up to, or until just before, a moment in the past: He was out of breath because he **had been running**. They deserved a rest — they **had been working** all morning.

rows: Tony had been studying **for** hours, so he had a headache.
She'd been hoping to win the competition **since** the summer.

notes: The past perfect simple often emphasises the result of an action: ✓ She'd **written** an article for the school newspaper. (= She'd finished it.)

The past perfect continuous often emphasises the action, and the time spent on the action, rather than the result: ✓ She'd **been writing** an article for the newspaper. (= She'd started, but she hadn't finished it.)

sections.title: A: Past perfect simple
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct past perfect simple form of the verbs in brackets.

items.sentence: By the time I arrived, everyone ...... (leave)!
Steve ...... (already / see) the film, so he didn't come with us to the cinema.
Tina ...... (not / finish) doing the housework by seven o'clock, so she called Andrea to tell her she would be late.
...... (you / just / speak) to Billy when I rang?
The car broke down just after ...... (we / set off).
I didn't eat anything at the party because ...... (I / already / eat) at home.
...... (you / hear) about the accident before you saw it on TV?

sections.title: B: Choose the sentence with the same meaning
sections.instructions: Choose the sentence (A or B) which means the same as the first sentence.

items.context: We'd had dinner when Wendy arrived.
items.sentenceA: Wendy arrived and then we had dinner.
items.sentenceB: We had dinner and then Wendy arrived.

items.context: I read the book after I'd seen the film.
items.sentenceA: I saw the film and then I read the book.
items.sentenceB: I read the book and then I saw the film.

items.context: By the time Dad came home, I'd gone to bed.
items.sentenceA: I went to bed before Dad came home.
items.sentenceB: I went to bed after Dad came home.

items.context: She didn't go to bed until her mum had come home.
items.sentenceA: She went to bed and then her mum came home.
items.sentenceB: Her mum came home and then she went to bed.

items.context: Mr Banks hadn't arrived at the office by the time I got there.
They'd bought the plane tickets before they heard about the cheaper flight.
items.sentenceA: They bought the plane tickets and later they heard about the cheaper flight.
items.sentenceB: They heard about the cheaper flight and then they bought the plane tickets.

items.context: The girls had tidied the house when the visitors arrived.
items.sentenceA: The visitors arrived and later the girls tidied the house.
items.sentenceB: The girls tidied the house and then the visitors arrived.

sections.title: C: Write sentences using the prompts
sections.instructions: Write sentences using the prompts. One of the verbs must be in the past perfect simple.

items.sentence: we / just / hear / the news / when / you / ring
items.answer: We had just heard the news when you rang.

items.sentence: I / already / think of / that / before / you / suggest / it
items.answer: I had already thought of that before you suggested it.

items.sentence: when / I / turn on / the TV / the programme / already / start
items.answer: When I turned on the TV, the programme had already started.

items.sentence: she / be / hungry / because / she / not / eat / anything / all day
items.answer: She was hungry because she hadn't eaten anything all day.

items.sentence: by the time / I / leave / school / I / decide / to become / a musician
items.answer: By the time I left school, I had decided to become a musician.

sections.title: D: Past perfect continuous
sections.instructions: Look at the pictures and complete the sentences. Use the correct form of the past perfect continuous.

items.sentence: She was tired because ...... (run).
They were hot because ...... (dance).
The garden was flooded because ...... (it / rain / all night).
items.answer: it had been raining all night

items.sentence: Did they crash because ...... (drive / too fast)?
items.answer: they had been driving too fast

items.sentence: When I arrived, ...... (they / wait / for over half an hour).
items.answer: they had been waiting for over half an hour

items.sentence: When I got there, ...... (they / not / wait / long).
I'd only ...... the washing-up for a few minutes when Clare came home, so she offered to finish it.
Had you already ...... James his birthday present when we gave him ours?
Gail hadn't ...... me that she would help me, so I wasn't angry when she didn't.
Mum had ...... her cup of tea for several minutes before she realised it had salt in it!
We'd ...... ready all day when they called to say the party had been cancelled.
It was a fantastic experience because I'd never ...... in a plane before.

sections.title: F: Find the extra word
sections.instructions: If a line is correct, click OK. If there is an extra word in a line, click on it.

items.sentence: This morning my exam results finally [had] came. I'd been expecting
them for the last week. I knew I'd [been] done quite well, but I was
still nervous as I [had] opened the envelope. Before I'd had a chance to
look at them, my sister ran up and pulled them out of my hand! She
[had] read them out one by one. 'English A, maths A, biology A,
French A ...' This was the news I'd been waiting for. I'd got As in
every subject – even geography, which I hadn't been making sure
about! When Mum and Dad heard the news, they immediately started
[been] shouting with joy. By the time I'd had breakfast, Mum had
already called Grandma and Grandpa and had [yet] told the neighbours!
